question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user flip the `owner` boolean parameter from `true` to `false` on a GET /api/v1/accounts/{id}/allowances/nfts request, causing the server to swap the JOOQ field from NFT_ALLOWANCE.SPENDER to NFT_ALLOWANCE.OWNER and return a completely different set of allowance records than the caller is entitled to view, effectively tampering with the apparent on-chain allowance state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `account.id=gte:0.0.0` range parameter to force the Bound constructor to set a lower bound of zero, causing the NftAllowanceRepository to return all NFT allowances across every account in the system, leaking the full on-chain allowance state submitted by all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user submit two conflicting `account.id` range parameters (e.g., `account.id=gte:0.0.1&account.id=lte:0.0.9999999`) to the Bound constructor to widen the query window far beyond the intended account, causing the service to return allowance records for thousands of unrelated accounts whose transactions were submitted to the ledger?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user set `limit=100` (MAX_LIMIT) combined with `account.id=gte:0.0.1` and `token.id=gte:0.0.1` to extract the maximum page of NFT allowance records in a single request, enabling bulk harvesting of submitted transaction allowance data without any rate-limiting or authentication check?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `order=desc` combined with `account.id=lte:0.0.9999999` to reverse the sort order and retrieve the most recently submitted NFT allowance transactions first, allowing targeted surveillance of the latest on-chain allowance grants without any access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an EVM address as the `{id}` path variable (e.g., `0x<40-hex-chars>`) to resolve a different account than the numeric ID would suggest, causing the EntityIdEvmAddressParameter branch to look up an account whose allowances were submitted under a different identity, effectively impersonating that account's allowance view?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a base32-encoded alias as the `{id}` path variable to resolve to an account whose numeric ID is unknown to the attacker, bypassing the expectation that callers must know the numeric account ID, and thereby accessing that account's submitted NFT allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `token.id=gt:0.0.0` parameter without supplying any `account.id` parameter, and observe whether the server throws `token.id parameter must have account.id present` or silently processes the request, to fingerprint the validation logic and plan a bypass of the account-scoping check on submitted allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `account.id=gte:0.0.1000&account.id=lte:0.0.999` (inverted range) to trigger the `Invalid range provided for account.id` exception in the Bound constructor, and by observing the exact error message, infer the internal range-validation logic to craft a subsequent request that narrowly avoids the check and retrieves allowance data outside the intended scope?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gt:0.0.1000&token.id=lt:0.0.800` (inverted token range) without a lower+upper account.id bound to bypass the `hasLowerAndUpper()` guard in `checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity`, causing the service to skip the token range validation and execute a query with an unintended token range against submitted allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user submit `account.id=ne:0.0.1000` to trigger the `Unsupported range operator ne` error in `Bound.verifyUnsupported()`, and use the verbose error message to enumerate which operators are supported, then craft a valid range query that extracts allowance records for accounts other than the path `{id}`, tampering with the intended account-scoped view of submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `owner=false` flag so that the EXTRACTORS map selects `nftAllowance.getOwner()` instead of `nftAllowance.getSpender()` for pagination link generation in `LinkFactoryImpl`, causing the next-page link to embed the owner's account ID rather than the spender's, and thereby allowing the attacker to follow pagination links that traverse allowances granted by a different account than the one in the path?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a pagination next-link returned by `LinkFactoryImpl.create()` by manually constructing the `account.id=gte:<value>&token.id=gt:<value>` query string, bypassing the server-generated link entirely, and use it to jump to an arbitrary page of NFT allowance records for any account, effectively tampering with the pagination cursor to access submitted transaction data out of sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=1` combined with a crafted `account.id=gte:<spender>` cursor to enumerate all NFT allowances one record at a time by following the `next` link in each response, effectively performing a full table scan of submitted NFT allowance transactions without any per-session or per-IP throttle?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `account.id=lte:0.0.9999999` without a lower bound so that the Bound sets `lower=null` and `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` returns 0, causing the repository query to scan from account 0 up to 9999999 and return all submitted NFT allowance transactions in that range, leaking the entire allowance state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gte:0.0.1` with a valid `account.id=gte:0.0.1` to satisfy the `Requires the presence of an gte or eq account.id parameter` check in `checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity`, and then retrieve all NFT allowances across all tokens from account 1 onward, effectively dumping all submitted allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=lte:0.0.9999999` with `account.id=lte:0.0.9999999` to satisfy the `Requires the presence of an lte or eq account.id parameter` check, and retrieve all NFT allowances in descending order across all accounts and tokens, effectively reversing through the entire submitted allowance transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a shard/realm/num triple (e.g., `0.0.1001`) as the `{id}` path variable and also supply `account.id=gte:0.0.1` to cause the EntityIdNumParameter to resolve the path account while the Bound independently queries a different account range, creating a mismatch between the path-scoped account and the filter-scoped accounts that leaks cross-account submitted allowance data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `account.id=0.0.1000` (no operator prefix, treated as EQ by EntityIdRangeParameter.valueOf()) combined with `owner=false` to pin the query to a specific spender account and retrieve all NFT allowances where that account is the spender, exposing the full set of submitted allowance grants made to that spender without any authentication?
